sourceMovie.title: The Exorcism of God
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Exorcism of God works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Exorcism of Emily Rose
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Exorcism of God clicked for you, The Exorcism of Emily Rose (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Shawshank Redemption
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Exorcism of God clicked for you, The Shawshank Redemption (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Edward Scissorhands
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Exorcism of God clicked for you, Edward Scissorhands (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Exorcism of God?
faq.answer: Try The Exorcism of Emily Rose, The Shawshank Redemption, Edward Scissorhands—each shares DNA with The Exorcism of God while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Exorcism of God?
faq.answer: The Exorcism of God (2022) is best described as Horror, Crime, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Exorcism of God worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
